messages.content:
comprehension checking questions for the following text:Steve is a politician who is always in the paper and is considered a local celebrity. He doesn’t mind being in the public eye and has become used to people watching everything he does. While he knows that being in the news is part of his job, he still feels that public officials deserve privacy in their personal lives and becomes upset when this is not respected. 
Steve decided to conduct a survey. The survey would ask about politicians and the contrast with openness in their public and private lives. 
When questioned about the survey Steve replied that he felt the facts would emerge soon and that they would support his belief in personal privacy.
When it was time to reveal the results of the survey, Steve found out that the public didn’t agree with him. The public felt that they had the right to know about his private life as well as his public life. Steve was shocked by the results, but decided to respect the people’s wishes and made his personal life more transparent.

1. Who is Steve?
2. How does Steve feel about being in the public eye?
3. What did Steve decide to do?
4. What did Steve believe the survey would reveal?
5. Did the results of the survey support Steve's belief in personal privacy?
6. How did the public feel about politicians' privacy?
7. How did Steve react to the results of the survey?
8. What did Steve decide to do after finding out the results of the survey?

find grammatical errors in text:Steve is a politician who is always in the paper and is considered a local celebrity. He doesn’t mind being in the public eye and has become used to people watching everything he does. While he knows that being in the news is part of his job, he still feels that public officials deserve privacy in their personal lives and becomes upset when this is not respected. 
Steve decided to conduct a survey. The survey would ask about politicians and the contrast with openness in their public and private lives. 
When questioned about the survey Steve replied that he felt the facts would emerge soon and that they would support his belief in personal privacy.
When it was time to reveal the results of the survey, Steve found out that the public didn’t agree with him. The public felt that they had the right to know about his private life as well as his public life. Steve was shocked by the results, but decided to respect the people’s wishes and made his personal life more transparent.
There are no grammatical errors in the text.
grammatical stress markers for:in the public eye
The grammatical stress marker in "in the public eye" is the preposition "in", and the noun "eye" is stressed in the phrase.
grammatical stress markers for each word in:in the public eye

"in" has primary stress on the first syllable "in".
"the" is an unstressed word.
"pub" has primary stress on the first syllable "pub".
"lic" has secondary stress on the second syllable "lic".
"eye" has primary stress on the first syllable "eye".
grammatical stress markers for each word in: would emerge soon

"would" has secondary stress on the first syllable "would".
"emerge" has primary stress on the second syllable "merge".
"soon" has primary stress on the only syllable "soon".
grammatical stress markers for each word in: reveal the results

"reveal" has primary stress on the second syllable "veal".
"the" is an unstressed word.
"results" has primary stress on the second syllable "sults".
highlight the subject + verb + pattern in: live in the public eye

Subject: "I" (implied - not explicitly stated but assumed to be the case)
Verb: "live"
Pattern: Subject + Verb + preposition phrase "in the public eye"
highlight the subject + verb + pattern in: deserve some privacy

Subject: "public officials"
Verb: "deserve"
Pattern: Subject + Verb + object "some privacy"
highlight the subject + verb + pattern in: conduct a survey

Subject: "Steve"
Verb: "conduct"
Pattern: Subject + Verb + object "a survey"
highlight the subject + verb + pattern in: contrast with
There is no complete sentence in "contrast with". It is a prepositional phrase.

suggestions for writing for B1 students:If something is going to emerge …
1) Has the information been concealed or undisclosed previously?(yes).
2)Is there a plan to maintain confidentiality?(no to make public)
3) Can we expect it to be introduced  suddenly?(yes)

Here's my suggestion for rephrasing the passage for B1 students:

"If something is going to emerge, it means that it was not known or not shared before. So the information was kept secret or hidden. However, there is no plan to keep it secret anymore. It will be made public, so everyone will know about it. You should also expect it to happen suddenly, without any prior announcement or warning."
B1 alternatives for concealed or undisclosed

Here are some B1 alternatives for "concealed or undisclosed":

- "Kept hidden or secret"
- "Not revealed or shared"
- "Maintained as private"
- "Not made public"
- "Hidden away or kept under wraps"
B1 alternative for maintain confidentiality

Here's a B1 alternative for "maintain confidentiality": 

- "Keep the information private"
B1 alternative for confidential before disclosure

Here's a B1 alternative for "confidential before disclosure":

- "Private information that wasn't shared before"
B1 alternative for undisclosed

Here's a B1 alternative for "undisclosed":

- "Not previously mentioned or revealed"
B1 alternative for keep it undisclosed

Here's a B1 alternative for "keep it undisclosed":

- "Keep it secret"

List the subject + verb + object in each:live/be in the public eye

Deserve some privacy

Conduct.  a  survey

Contrast strongly with

Results will Emerge soon

Reveal the results of the survey

- live/be (verb) in the public eye (object)
- deserve (verb) some privacy (object)
- conduct (verb) a survey (object)
- contrast (verb) strongly with (object)
- emerge (verb) soon (object)
- reveal (verb) the results of the survey (object)
